exemplar.title: To what extent does Arginine Vasopressin influence adult relationships?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent does Arginine Vasopressin influence adult relationships?

subject.title: Psychology
subject.description:
IB Psychology is a Group 3 subject in the International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP). The course examines human behavior and mental processes through scientific inquiry, exploring biological, cognitive, and sociocultural approaches to understanding behavior. Students develop critical thinking skills while investigating contemporary psychological research, research methods, and ethical considerations in psychological practice.

The course is available at Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours) and Higher Level (HL, 240 hours), with first assessment in 2027.

Assessment consists of Paper 1 (integration of concepts, content, and contexts through short-answer and extended-response questions: SL 2 hours, HL 2 hours, SL 50%, HL 40%), Paper 2 (application of psychological knowledge to research contexts: SL 1 hour, HL 2 hours, SL 25%, HL 20%), Paper 3 (HL only: research methods and data analysis, 1 hour, 20%), and Internal Assessment (research proposal development: SL 25%, HL 20%).

strengths: Clear and focused research question prominently stated and maintained throughout the report
Purposeful selection of relevant peer-reviewed studies with explicit inclusion criteria
Introduction and chapter transitions effectively frame the scope and maintain narrative flow
weaknesses: Table of contents page numbering is imprecise, hindering easy navigation
Occasional minor repetition of the research question disrupts fluidity
Future research recommendations lack specific experimental design details
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work consistently communicates topic, focus and methodology at a level befitting band 5. The research question is clearly formulated and tightly linked to discussion. Methodological choices are well justified and varied. Minor lapses in table-of-contents accuracy and occasional repetitions prevent a full band-6 award, but the overall clarity, coherence and methodological rigor clearly exceed band-4 standards.

strengths: Excellent integration of subject-specific terminology (e.g., âconstruct validityâ, âintranasal administrationâ)
Consistent and accurate application of biological and psychological concepts throughout
Robust source application with up-to-date peer-reviewed studies coherently woven into argumentation
weaknesses: A few minor factual inaccuracies (e.g., mis-dating of Taylor et al.)
Occasional stylistic slips in terminology formatting (e.g., âsamesexâ)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The submission demonstrates very strong subject knowledge and consistently accurate use of specialized terminology, meriting band-5. All cited studies and theoretical constructs are correctly applied to support the investigation. Minor factual inaccuracies and occasional stylistic lapses prevent a perfect band-6 rating, but the work clearly surpasses band-4 expectations.

strengths: Well-structured, critical evaluation of validity, sampling and causality across diverse studies
Effective synthesis of findings into a coherent argument with supported conclusions
Insightful discussion of methodological limitations (e.g., ecological validity, retrospective bias)
weaknesses: Cross-species generalizability of animal models is not fully critiqued
Limited exploration of socio-cultural moderators in gender-difference analysis
Sample size and statistical power considerations could be addressed more deeply
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The investigation displays an advanced level of critical thinking. Research is consistently relevant, analysis is deep and conclusions are well supported. The argument is structured and evaluative, with thoughtful consideration of validity threats and limitations. Only minor gapsâsuch as deeper socio-cultural critique and cross-species generalizabilityâkeep the work from the absolute top mark, but overall performance clearly belongs in the 10â12 band.

strengths: Logical EE structure with clear title page, introduction, themed chapters and conclusion
Consistent use of APA-style headings and in-text citations
weaknesses: Table of contents page references are incorrect (all â1â), reducing navigability
Minor inconsistencies in reference list formatting
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The presentation meets core EE conventions and demonstrates clear structure. However, functional navigational elementsâaccurate table of contents and page numbersâare missing, and reference styling is uneven in places. This merits a solid but not full-mark performance in presentation.

strandSelections.reasoning: Psychology terminologyââconstruct validityâ, âtheory of mindâ, âintranasal administrationâ, âcorrugator EMGââis used accurately and in context throughout.  Concepts such as internal/external validity and reductionism are applied correctly.  A few stylistic slips (e.g., âsamesexâ without space) are present but do not detract from an otherwise precise and consistent technical register.

Formatting elements are generally present: title page with word count, section headings, in-text citations, reference list.  However, page numbers appear absent, table-of-contents pagination is inaccurate, and minor inconsistencies in reference styling are evident.  These lapses mean some but not all layout conventions are met, warranting a placement in the 1-2 band with 2 marks.

The essay demonstrates solid command of psychological and biological content: AVP pathways, empathy constructs, attachment theory, study designs, validity types, etc.  Source material is invariably academic and correctly integrated into argumentation.  Minor inaccuracies (e.g., mis-dating Taylor et al., occasional conflation of cognitive/emotional empathy) prevent a flawless rating but knowledge remains consistently strong.

The student selects a purposeful range of peer-reviewed human and animal studies (genetic, hormonal-administration, behavioural, physiological) and applies clear criteria for inclusion (gender balance, methodology, relevance).  Each study is summarised and critiqued, showing informed selection.  Because evidence is exclusively secondary literature rather than primary data collection, the methodological description is thorough but not fully âcompleteâ; consequently the strand is placed at 5 rather than 6.

The title, research question and introductory section give a clear, concise statement of purpose (examining the impact of AVP on adult relationships).  Key theoretical frames (attachment theory, ToM, empathy) are set out and linked explicitly to the biological focus, allowing the reader to understand both scope and direction.  Minor weaknesses â e.g., an imprecise table-of-contents page numbering and occasional repetition of the question â do not seriously hinder communication.  Overall clarity and focus correspond with the upper band but small lapses keep it to the lower end of 5-6.

The investigation draws on a good breadth of contemporary, peer-reviewed research directly relevant to the RQ.  Studies are recent, diverse in method and clearly linked to adult social or romantic functioning, showing purposeful, mostly relevant selection.  A few secondary citations and limited cultural diversity in sources lower comprehensiveness, situating quality at the lower end of the 7-9 band.

The research question is explicitly stated in the title block and reiterated throughout.  It is appropriately narrow (one neuropeptide, adult relationships) and consistently referenced in each analytical section, demonstrating firm alignment between question and discussion.  Occasional digressions into oxytocin or broader social factors are acknowledged as limitations and do not dilute focus, justifying a strong but not perfect performance.

The essay follows a logical EE formatâtitle page, contents, introduction, themed chapters, conclusion, references.  Headings are clear and APA-style references are provided.  Weaknesses include a non-functional table of contents (all pages marked â1â), missing page numbers in the extract, and limited subdivision within major sections, but the overall structure still meets core conventions.

The discussion moves from social bonding to romantic relationships and ends with a balanced conclusion that weighs gender differences, methodological limits and reductionism.  Evaluation addresses validity, sampling, and causality, showing structure and critical stance.  Nevertheless, some assertions (e.g., possible threats posed by sex-word recognition) are speculative, and alternative explanations beyond biology are only briefly noted, keeping the work in the 7 range rather than higher.

Each study is analysed for design, sample, validity, and theoretical implications; comparisons are drawn across findings, and gender-specific effects are synthesised.  Conclusions are generally supported by evidence, though causal claims are occasionally overstated and some critiques become descriptive.  Depth is therefore solid but not uniformly penetrating, meriting the lower level of the 7-9 range.

annotations.comment: The student correctly states the investigationâs title at the start of the report, meeting the requirement for a title block.
The research question is clearly and prominently stated, demonstrating precise focus on AVPâs influence on adult relationships.
The word count is within the 4000-word limit and is correctly placed on the title page, fulfilling formatting requirements.
Page numbers are absent from the document, hindering navigation and violating layout conventions.
Table of contents entries all reference page 1, making the ToC non-functional and confusing for readers.
Consider updating the table of contents with accurate page references and ensuring dynamic numbering.
The introduction effectively frames AVPâs relevance by detailing both animal and human studies, clarifying the topicâs scope.
The reliance on animal models is not critiqued for cross-species generalizability; adding this would strengthen the analysis.
Key theoretical constructs (attachment theory, ToM, STE) are integrated clearly, demonstrating sound subject understanding.
The concluding sentence sets up the essay structure and restates the RQ, reinforcing focus.
The chapter introduction outlines methodological breadth (correlational to laboratory), clearly justifying source selection.
The summary of Buffone and Poulinâs genetic study is detailed and shows excellent integration of biological and psychological measures.
Explicitly link each studyâs methodological justification back to the research question to underscore relevance.
The results are presented with clarity, detailing AVPR1a interactions and gender differences precisely.
The claim that internal validity is low then higher is contradictory; streamline the evaluation for coherence.
Correctly identifies high construct validity through use of established behavioral and genetic measures.
The critique of retrospective self-report bias is insightful, demonstrating an understanding of potential validity threats.
When proposing further investigation, specify the experimental design and controls that would address current limitations.
Uzefovsky et al.âs double-blind design and RMET use are methodologically robust, enhancing internal and construct validity.
The analysis correctly notes AVPâs selective impairment for negative emotions, reflecting depth of interpretation.
Discuss the impact of the modest sample size on statistical power and generalizability.
The background on Thompson et al. highlights multi-modal measures (EMG, ratings), demonstrating sophisticated methodological understanding.
The discussion of ecological validity succinctly acknowledges limitations of lab settings on generalizability.
A more detailed exploration of socio-cultural moderators would enrich the discussion of gender differences.
The chapter transition into romantic relationships is smooth, sustaining narrative flow and focus.
Taylor et al.âs correlational design and triangulation of self-report and AVP assays are well-described and justified.
The limitations section is thorough in addressing validity threats, demonstrating critical evaluation.
Suggest specifying neuroimaging methods (e.g., fMRI, EEG) in future research proposals to bolster methodological clarity.
Guastella et al.âs word-recognition task is described with precision, showing strong grasp of experimental design.
The conclusion chapter synthesizes gender-specific AVP effects effectively, weaving evidence into a coherent overarching argument.
Future research recommendations are insightful but would benefit from specific timelines or feasibility considerations.
Reference list is comprehensive and current, demonstrating excellent source selection and application.
Ensure consistent APA styling (italics, punctuation) across all reference entries to enhance presentation quality.
